title: express-jwt with credentialsRequired: false — routes that look protected accept requests with NO token

params.passDetail: express-jwt requires credentials (credentialsRequired is not false) — a request without a valid token is rejected before it reaches the protected handler.
params.failDetail: express-jwt is configured with credentialsRequired: false, so requests that carry NO Authorization token are allowed through instead of being rejected. Every route behind this middleware silently becomes optional-auth: an anonymous caller reaches the handler, and any code that assumes req.auth exists runs unauthenticated. Only set credentialsRequired: false on genuinely public routes; protected routes must leave it at the default (true).
params.absentCallDetail: No expressjwt() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: expressjwt() does not set credentialsRequired; the secure default (true) applies.

provenance.note: credentialsRequired: false is meant for mixed public/private routes, but pasted onto a blanket app.use it turns an entire API's auth into optional. The app still returns data, so the missing enforcement is invisible until someone notices unauthenticated access works.
